nodes.title: 5.1 Tailoring for Every Application
nodes.text:
<physical_index_3>
## 5.1 Tailoring for Every Application

- Read the job description twice. First pass: understand the role. Second pass: extract the top 6–8 required skills, tools, and outcomes.
- Mirror JD language in your summary, skills, and top 2–3 bullets. Not paraphrased — exact terms. ATS matches strings.
- Reorder your skills section so the most JD-relevant category appears first.
- Swap in 1–2 role-specific bullets you have prepared in a 'backup bullet bank' — a separate document where you keep 3–5 additional bullets per role that didn't fit the one-page constraint.
- Rewrite your summary for every application. This is 10 minutes of work. It is also the single highest-ROI activity in job searching.
- Time investment per application: 15–20 minutes of tailoring is the difference between a 2% and a 25% response rate.
<physical_index_3>

nodes.title: 5.2 The 10-Second Scan Test
nodes.text:
<physical_index_3>
## 5.2 The 10-Second Scan Test

- Open your resume. Set a 10-second timer. Look at it fresh. What registers immediately?
- Your name, current title, and most recent company should be instantly legible without searching.
- At least one metric (%, $, scale) should be visible in the first 3 bullets of work experience without zooming.
- If the page looks like a grey block of text — restructure. More white space, shorter bullets, bolder company names.
- Have one person who doesn't know your career history read it for 10 seconds and tell you what you do. If they can't, the summary and top section need rewriting.
<physical_index_3>

nodes.title: 5.3 Pre-Submission Checklist
nodes.text:
<physical_index_3>
## 5.3 Pre-Submission Checklist
<physical_index_3>

<physical_index_4>
✓ One page (or genuinely justified two pages for 8+ year profiles).
✓ No typos — spell-check and then manually read every line, top to bottom.
✓ Consistent tense: present for current role, past tense for all previous roles.
✓ Every bullet leads with an action verb. Zero instances of 'Responsible for' or 'Worked on'.
✓ Every bullet contains at least one quantifier or meaningful scope qualifier.
✓ Date format consistent throughout (e.g., 'Jan 2022' — not mixing 'January 2022' and '01/22').
✓ LinkedIn URL is customized and the profile is live and complete.
✓ PDF opens correctly and maintains formatting on both desktop and mobile.
✓ File named: FirstName_LastName_Resume.pdf.
✓ Top 6 keywords from the target JD appear naturally in the resume.
<physical_index_4>

nodes.title: 5.4 Universal Resume Mistakes
nodes.text:
<physical_index_4>
## 5.4 Universal Resume Mistakes

|  ❌Stop Doing This | ✓ Do This Instead  |
| --- | --- |
|  Objective statement: 'Seeking a challenging role where I can grow'
Skill bars, star ratings, or proficiency percentages
'Responsible for managing...' / 'Worked on...'
Duties listed without a single outcome or result
No numbers anywhere — no %, $, time saved, or scale
Identical resume sent to every job application
Three-page resume with two years of experience
'References available upon request' — wastes space
Soft skills section: 'Teamwork, Communication, Leadership'
Random bold text used for emphasis instead of scannability | 3-line tailored summary: title + proof metric + direction
Grouped skill categories, clean comma-separated list
Strong past-tense action verb + specific outcome
STAR-format bullets: action + scope + quantified result
Every role has at least one number: revenue, %, users, time
15-minute tailored version per application, every time
1 tight, dense, high-signal page — 2 only if truly warranted
Remove it entirely — it occupies prime real estate
Let the bullets prove soft skills — never claim them
Bold only: company names, job titles, key tech anchors  |

PART 6 — PROFESSIONAL SUMMARY (SDE / SWE / Cloud)
<physical_index_4>

nodes.title: 6.1 What Recruiters Look For in an SDE Summary
nodes.text:
<physical_index_4>
## 6.1 What Recruiters Look For in an SDE Summary

- Stack clarity: what languages and systems do you live in? Recruiters scan for their required tech in the first sentence.
- Scale signal: how large were the systems you built or maintained? Users, requests/day, data volume, or team size.
- Impact framing: performance gains, reliability improvements, or shipping velocity — not just 'built features'.
- Seniority signal: junior resumes show 'built X using Y'; senior resumes show 'designed, scaled, and owned X serving Y'.
<physical_index_4>

nodes.title: 6.2 Summary Formula for SDE / Cloud
nodes.text:
<physical_index_4>
## 6.2 Summary Formula for SDE / Cloud

[X-year] [role: Backend/Full-Stack/Cloud/Platform Engineer] with expertise in [primary stack: language + framework + infra].
[Strongest achievement: what you built/optimized + measurable outcome: latency %, uptime, cost, users served].
Focused on [system design area: distributed systems / reliability / developer productivity] for [team type: product-led / infra / platform] teams.
<physical_index_4>

nodes.title: 8.1 Role Header Format
nodes.text:
<physical_index_5>
## 8.1 Role Header Format
<physical_index_5>

<physical_index_6>
Software Engineer | Company Name (Series B SaaS, 150 employees) | Jan 2022 – Present
City, Country (or 'Remote')

- Add company context in parentheses if the company is not well-known: stage (Series B), sector (fintech), and size (150 employees). One line, parenthetical.
- Most recent role: 4–5 bullets. Previous roles: 2–3 bullets. Roles older than 7 years: 1–2 bullets or consider removing.
- Promote to top: the one bullet from each role that most directly mirrors the JD you're targeting.
<physical_index_6>

nodes.title: 8.2 The Impact Bullet Framework
nodes.text:
<physical_index_6>
## 8.2 The Impact Bullet Framework

STRUCTURE: [Action Verb] + [What + How (technical specifics)] + [Result with metric]

EXAMPLE: Redesigned + the authentication service (Node.js + Redis session store) + cutting P95 login latency from 1.8s to 340ms for 800K MAU.
<physical_index_6>

nodes.title: 8.3 The Four Metric Dimensions for SDE
nodes.text:
<physical_index_6>
## 8.3 The Four Metric Dimensions for SDE

- PERFORMANCE: Latency (P50/P95/P99), throughput (req/sec, events/day), error rate (%), uptime (99.9% → 99.99%). These are the most credible engineering metrics.
- SCALE: Users (DAU/MAU), data volume (TB/day), traffic (RPS), infrastructure size (pods, nodes, services). Scale signals that your experience is production-grade.
- COST: Infrastructure savings ($K/year), compute reduction (%), licensing eliminations. Financial impact resonates with both engineering managers and executives.
- VELOCITY: Deployment frequency (releases/week), CI pipeline time reduction (minutes), test coverage increase (%), PR cycle time. These signal engineering culture contribution.
- When you cannot find a hard metric: use scope. 'Used by 8 internal teams', 'adopted as the org-wide standard', 'reduced on-call incidents from 12/week to 2/week'.
<physical_index_6>

nodes.title: 8.4 Strong Action Verb Bank (SDE)
nodes.text:
<physical_index_6>
## 8.4 Strong Action Verb Bank (SDE)

Architected • Engineered • Designed • Built • Implemented • Shipped • Deployed • Scaled • Optimized • Refactored • Migrated • Automated • Instrumented • Debugged • Integrated • Containerized • Orchestrated • Provisioned • Benchmarked • Open-sourced
<physical_index_6>

nodes.title: 8.5 Before vs. After — SDE Bullets
nodes.text:
<physical_index_6>
## 8.5 Before vs. After — SDE Bullets

|  ❌Vague (Never Write These) | ✓ Specific (Write These Instead)  |
| --- | --- |
|  Worked on backend APIs for the product | Built 12 REST endpoints (Node.js + PostgreSQL) for the core order management service, reducing client-side data fetching by 60%  |
|  Responsible for database optimization | Reindexed 3 high-traffic PostgreSQL tables and rewrote 5 N+1 queries, cutting average API response time from 1.4s to 210ms  |
|  Helped migrate services to the cloud | Migrated 4 monolithic services to AWS Lambda + API Gateway, reducing infrastructure cost by $38K/year and eliminating 2 on-call incidents/week  |
|  Fixed bugs and improved code quality | Eliminated 220 production bugs across 3 sprints by introducing automated Jest + Supertest coverage from 4% to 81%  |
|  Wrote unit tests for the application | Increased unit and integration test coverage from 12% to 79%, reducing production regression incidents by 65% over two quarters  |
|  Worked in an Agile development team | Led adoption of 2-week sprint cadence for a 6-engineer team, improving on-time delivery rate from 54% to 91% over two quarters  |
<physical_index_6>

nodes.title: 8.6 Demonstrating Seniority Through Bullets
nodes.text:
<physical_index_6>
## 8.6 Demonstrating Seniority Through Bullets

- Junior: 'Built X feature using Y technology' — shows execution ability.
- Mid-level: 'Designed and built X, improving Y by Z%' — shows ownership and impact.
- Senior: 'Defined architecture for X, led 3-engineer squad to deliver in 6 weeks, reducing Y by Z%' — shows leadership, design judgment, and measurable delivery.
<physical_index_6>

<physical_index_7>
- Staff/Principal: 'Set technical direction for X across 4 teams, establishing org-wide standard that reduced Y by Z%' — shows org-level influence.
- The gap between junior and senior bullets is not the vocabulary — it's the scope of ownership expressed.

---
<physical_index_7>
